description:
A machine-learning engineer in your team needs a small, tidy SQLite
    data-mart to feed into a preprocessing pipeline.  
    Your objective is to prepare that data set entirely from the Linux
    command line, using the sqlite3 CLI that is already installed in the
    environment.

    Starting point  
    1. A CSV file is already on-disk at
       /home/user/data/raw_reviews.csv

    Required end state  
    1. Create a brand-new SQLite database file at
       /home/user/workspace/reviews.db
       • The file must live inside the already-existing
         /home/user/workspace directory (create the directory if it does
         not yet exist).  
    2. Import /home/user/data/raw_reviews.csv as a table named
       reviews inside that database.  
       • The table must have exactly four columns, in this order
         (all TEXT except the first, which is INTEGER):
           review_id  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
           product_id TEXT  
           sentiment  TEXT  
           review_text TEXT  
       • After the import, the table must contain every row that appears
         in the source CSV.  
    3. Inside the same database, create a new table called
       summary_stats that contains two columns:
         sentiment  TEXT  
         cnt        INTEGER  
       • Populate it with one row per distinct sentiment value that
         appears in the reviews table.  
       • The cnt column must store the number of reviews that share that
         sentiment.  
    4. Export the summary_stats table to a comma-separated file named
       /home/user/workspace/review_summary.csv  
       • The CSV must include a single header row exactly equal to:
           sentiment,cnt  
       • Each data row must be on its own line and contain the two
         fields separated by one comma and no extra whitespace.  
       • The order of the data rows does not matter.  
    5. Produce a small human-readable log in  
       /home/user/workspace/database_setup.log that shows each major
       milestone you completed.  
       • It must contain exactly four lines, in this precise order:
           STEP: database created
           STEP: reviews table imported (6 rows)
           STEP: summary_stats table generated
           STEP: summary exported -> /home/user/workspace/review_summary.csv  

    Success verification – what the automated tests will look for  
    • The file /home/user/workspace/reviews.db exists and is a valid
      SQLite database.  
    • Running   sqlite3 /home/user/workspace/reviews.db ".tables"
      returns a list containing both reviews and summary_stats.  
    • SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reviews; yields 6  
      (the exact number of rows present in the source CSV).  
    • SELECT * FROM summary_stats; returns exactly three rows with the
      correct sentiment labels and counts (the sentiment labels are
      case-sensitive).  
    • The file /home/user/workspace/review_summary.csv exists, contains
      the header sentiment,cnt followed by three rows, and no extra
      blank lines or whitespace.  
    • The file /home/user/workspace/database_setup.log exists and its
      contents are byte-for-byte identical to the four lines specified
      above (including capitalisation, punctuation and spacing).  

    IMPORTANT  
    • Do not hard-code the sentiment counts; derive them from the table
      you import so the solution works even if the CSV changes.  
    • All work must be done from the Linux shell (bash) using the
      sqlite3 command-line tool—no Python or other external languages.  
    • You do NOT need root privileges at any point, and you must not
      attempt to obtain them.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing artefacts -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/raw_reviews.csv"><![CDATA[
review_id,product_id,sentiment,review_text
1,P001,positive,"Great product, works flawlessly."
2,P002,negative,"Stopped working after a week."
3,P001,positive,"Absolutely love it!"
4,P003,neutral,"It is okay, does the job."
5,P002,negative,"Poor build quality."
6,P004,positive,"Exceeded my expectations!"
    ]]></file>

    <!-- Expected files created by the agent -->
    <file path="/home/user/workspace/database_setup.log"><![CDATA[
STEP: database created
STEP: reviews table imported (6 rows)
STEP: summary_stats table generated
STEP: summary exported -> /home/user/workspace/review_summary.csv
    ]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/workspace/review_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
sentiment,cnt
negative,2
neutral,1
positive,3
    ]]></file>

    <!-- Database verification queries -->
    <db path="/home/user/workspace/reviews.db">
        <expect>
            .tables -> contains "reviews" and "summary_stats"
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reviews; -> 6
            SELECT * FROM summary_stats ORDER BY sentiment; -> [
                ('negative',2),
                ('neutral',1),
                ('positive',3)
            ]
        </expect>
    </db>
name: task_000000_3c64230a
